description: DIAGNOSTIC: Capture browser DevTools Network tab data when qwen3 block happens. Need to see what browser actually received — response headers, body, timing. This is the key missing piece to understand why SSL_write(565B) succeeds but JS shows 'Thinking' spinner.

services.test_prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 900101-1234567 입니다. 이 번호가 올바른 형식인지 확인해주세요.
services.capture_instructions: BEFORE submitting prompt: Open DevTools (F12) → Network tab → check 'Preserve log'. Then submit the prompt. After 'Thinking' spinner appears and stays for 30+ seconds, capture: (1) Screenshot of Network tab showing the chat API request, (2) Click on the chat API request and show Response tab — what does the browser show as response body? (3) Show Headers tab — request/response headers. (4) If possible, export HAR file.
services.expected: We need to see: (a) Does the browser receive the 565-byte block response? (b) What status code does the browser show? (c) Is there a response body? (d) Any connection errors in the request entry?
services.notes: Phase3-B25 deployed. APF sends HTTP/1.1 200 OK with text/event-stream + SSE data. VTS confirms written=565. But browser shows stuck spinner.

notes: 4 consecutive tests (#363, #365, #366, #368) show STUCK_ON_THINKING. VTS delivery confirmed. Browser-side evidence is the missing diagnostic. Also: #367 proved GOAWAY hypothesis wrong — connection killed entirely. We need to understand the browser-MITM interaction.
